title: Separate combinational from sequential logic
description: Model sequential state in always_ff with non-blocking assignments and combinational logic in always_comb with blocking, never mixing the two.
body: Use when writing or reviewing any RTL block. Do not mix blocking (=) and non-blocking (<=) in the same clocked process, and do not compute next-state logic inside a flip-flop block. Actions: put register updates in always_ff with <=, put next-state/output logic in always_comb with =, and inspect each block to confirm exactly one driver per signal. Validation signals include a lint pass with no blocking-in-sequential warnings and matching RTL vs gate-level simulation. Risk boundary: a blocking assignment in sequential logic creates a simulation race that can diverge in silicon; treat it as a blocking defect, not a style nit.
